item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it's a photo of a sculpture by Myeongbeom Kim (as seen here) with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This heavily reminds me of the langoliers, and that's great because the langoliers are absolutely terrifying

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YOUR April only has 30 days!

fixed, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Date of death determined to be 04/31/20██

you realize April only has 30 days, yes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad I decided to check on this today, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Space within SCP-2249 is looped internally; any subjects approaching SCP-2249's border will find themselves on the opposite border, with no signs of transition.

There's two different versions of this line in the same paragraph at different spots in the Description. I suggest taking one of them out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, edited a few things, added a few others. I think it holds together a bit more now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, a lot. I feel very sympathetic for the doctor. So sad that he wanted to improve the children's lives, only for it to fail miserably.

Upvoted +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another example: the article goes from saying, "Multiple constructions can be seen," to then later mentioning off-hand that there's a town there. Saying there are "multiple constructions" is different from saying that there's an entire town. And yeah, I've been putting off writing an exploration log until I can get some better ideas for it.

Ah see, I think you misread this part. The town is outside of the actual extra-dimensional space; the hospital was inside the town. And yeah, I've been putting off writing an exploration log until I can get some better ideas for it.

As for the rest of your critique, I did find it difficult to balance between clinical and interesting, if I failed in a few areas that doesn't surprise me. I'm going to rewrite some of this at some point if I can ever stop being lazy. I do appreciate the feedback, so thanks!

And, if you want to see the bloated version, check out my sandbox. Some of the older versions are very long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor nitpicks:

"The exception to this is a 1.2 km long pathway leading from the elevator entrance to SCP-2249-1," - sentence ends in a comma instead of a period.

"Entrance to SCP-2249 is through an elevator on the first floor of the hospital, in which is a single tree (Pseudotsuga menziesii) on a bed of grass." - Why is there a species name for the tree, but not the grass?

Major nitpicks:

The concept is interesting, but it's got way too much telling instead of showing with regards to the contents of the dimension. What I mean is, you sort of say, "Here's a thing, and here's another thing, and here's another thing," without going into any real detail about them. It stops being atmospheric, stops letting us explore a new world, and starts feeling like a list. I'd like to see the bloated version of this, I think it'd be better, because right now it's paired down too much.

+ show block
– hide block

The biggest offender is the "apparent tears" in space paragraph, in which the article rattles off that there are tears in space "apparently." Wait what? What does that mean? What does a tear in space look like that you can say that something "apparently" is one? The article rattles off that there are holes in reality with the same tone as saying that there's a bed of grass. Was this written by someone who sees tears in space every day with the same frequency as grass?

Another example: the article goes from saying, "Multiple constructions can be seen," to then later mentioning off-hand that there's a town there. Saying there are "multiple constructions" is different from saying that there's an entire town. That wasn't clear. There's a lot of details that get glossed over, like you're in a rush to get to the next thing. Notice how the entire town this guy constructed gets as lengthy a description as the interior of the elevator. Take your time, the appeal of this SCP is the bizarre alternate universe.

This would be remedied either with an exploration log or just going into more detail about the contents of the dimension, especially the town and the house.

Also, the video logs of the scientist currently add nothing to the story. The concept of a failed dreamland for children turning into a radioactive nightmare zone is fertile ground for tragedy, and making us feel for the doctor and his wishes to create a paradise for children would add a great emotional punch, but the logs just sort of repeat what we already know.

No vote. It's a great concept, but the execution needs a lot of retooling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah OK. I have an idea of how large the space is anyways, so this wouldn't be terribly difficult to implement. I was planning on doing an Exploration Log regardless, so I may just change it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much that.

Could even use a wheeled, shielded drone with a shielded umbilical; basically a glorified RC car with a (long) wired controller and lead plating around its electronics. Heavy Coaxial or Triaxial Cable would probably work well enough for getting a decent signal punched through over moderate ranges. It wouldn't be very agile or sending back hd imagery, and you'd be constantly having to weave it around trees1 but you could at least get the sort of data you list about side locations and gamma readings, and do some basic triangulation mapping; figure out how big the thing is and where things are in relation to each other.

Not something you necessarily need to put in, but it's a thought.

Footnotes
1. wouldn't get caught on them as long as the spool was on the drone, but it'd still be a challange to deal with

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. Well I suppose you could have someone estimate how large it is, but walking out into it would be a bad idea for obvious reasons… I guess you could throws a D class on a rope and see how far he gets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.
Just the right mixture of science, and tragedy to make it good. Well done.

I have one question though. If mapping the area using electronic means is impossible, couldn't it be done by more basic means, as in simple human observation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't think I've ever seen it done before. Every other place and building is always "whoops camera glitched" just 'cause. It was a good surprise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, alright. Its an interesting enough premise that I can get behind.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh OK,I took it as sarcastic. Damn you lack of sarcasm script!

And that's fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not a bad thing. He's saying you did it right.

He's just no-voting for other reasons (which is fine).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, really? How is it a bad thing to explain that gamma radiation interferes with electronics quite a bit!? It's not as though I added the radiation just to stop ytech from working, I researched it and found that could happen afterwards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god, you guys!

He explained why tech doesn't work in the spooky place!

Otherwise, fine, whatever, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was incredibly bloated in it's first iteration, and while I've trimmed it down a lot it is a bit long. I still feel like everything in here needs to be though.

And would you mind elaborating on what could use more explaining? Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Edit: Also, thanks for cleaning stuff up, I knew I wouldn't get it all.

Something I forgot to say, I'm not entirely sure what you mean by "too expository". Like, it's too much like someone telling a story, or more like too much explaining what's going on rather than a journal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels a bit bloated, and I feel like it doesn't quite explain the bits that need to be explained. As well, the writing in the journals doesn't quite feel natural enough. It all feels too expository, in my opinion.

There are also a number of errors with quotation marks, bolding, and more… I fixed up all that I saw, but there could be more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember this!

You've taken it further. Excellent. Pulling together the idea into a much more cohesive whole. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The show/shows conversation was the worst thing I've ever been a part of in 19. +1

item.title: Re: First Scp
item.content:encoded:
Hehehe

*pinches self*
Flora, not fauna, idiot.

Anyways, I never got to see the full draft, and I have to mention how much I love spatially-ubiquitous structures. An enthusiastic upvote from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Strange. I feel like I've read this before.

/shrugs

Maybe it was a dream. Have an upvote.

item.title: First Scp
item.content:encoded:
Welp, here it is, "The Failed Dreamland." Hope it goes well.

Thanks to Petrograd for their critique on the forums, really helped with the develpoment of this idea.

Also, thanks to people in the IRC for some help, and teaching me about "shows" vs "show" for a singular noun.
